name: Complaint for Breach of Purchase Agreement
summary: Drafts a comprehensive complaint alleging breach of a purchase agreement in real estate litigation. Establishes jurisdiction, venue, parties, and compliance with court rules and formatting requirements. Use when initiating a lawsuit as plaintiff against a party breaching a real property purchase contract.
content:
# Complaint for Breach of Purchase Agreement

You are drafting a comprehensive litigation complaint alleging breach of a purchase agreement. This document must comply with all applicable court rules, state procedural requirements, and professional standards for pleading practice. Your goal is to produce a well-structured, factually supported complaint that clearly establishes the elements of breach of contract and positions the plaintiff for successful litigation.

## Document Structure and Requirements

Begin by establishing the proper caption and court information. Identify the specific court where the action will be filed, including the full legal name of the court, division if applicable, and case number if one has been assigned. Present the plaintiff and defendant with their complete legal names, addresses, and any relevant business entity designations. Research the specific formatting requirements for the jurisdiction where this complaint will be filed, as courts often have particular rules regarding caption format, font requirements, line spacing, and margin specifications. Consult official court websites, local rules, and state bar association resources to ensure compliance with these technical requirements.

## Jurisdiction and Venue Analysis

Draft a clear and legally sufficient statement of jurisdiction and venue. Articulate the basis for the court's subject matter jurisdiction, whether it derives from diversity of citizenship, the amount in controversy, or state law grounds. Examine the purchase agreement thoroughly to identify any forum selection or choice of law clauses that may dictate where the action must be brought or what law applies. If such clauses exist, address them directly and explain how they support filing in the chosen forum. Verify your jurisdictional allegations against authoritative legal sources to ensure they meet the standards established by controlling statutes and case law. The venue statement should explain why the chosen court is the proper location for this action, referencing relevant factors such as where the contract was executed, where performance was to occur, where the breach occurred, or where the defendant resides or conducts business.

## Parties Section

Provide detailed identification of all parties to the litigation. For the plaintiff, include the full legal name, current address, and a description of their role in the purchase agreement (such as buyer, seller, or assignee). Similarly identify the defendant with complete information including any trade names, business entity type, registered agent information if applicable, and their role in the underlying transaction. If either party is a business entity, specify the type of entity (corporation, LLC, partnership, etc.) and the state of formation or incorporation. Review best practices from reputable legal resources regarding how to properly describe parties in breach of contract complaints, particularly when dealing with complex business entities, multiple parties, or situations involving successors or assigns.

## Factual Background and Narrative

Construct a detailed chronological narrative that tells the complete story of the transaction and subsequent breach. Begin with the circumstances that led to the formation of the purchase agreement, including any negotiations, preliminary agreements, or representations made during the formation process. Describe the purchase agreement itself with specificity: identify the date of execution, the subject matter of the purchase (whether real property, business assets, goods, or other property), the purchase price and payment terms, and all material terms and conditions. Extract specific language, dates, dollar amounts, and obligations directly from the purchase agreement and any related documents. Present the plaintiff's performance under the agreement, detailing what obligations were fulfilled, when payments were made, and what conditions were satisfied. Then describe the defendant's breach with precision: identify exactly what obligation was not performed, when the breach occurred, what notice was given, and what opportunities for cure were provided. Include specific dates, communications, and factual details that demonstrate both the existence of the contract and the defendant's failure to perform. This narrative should be thorough enough that a reader unfamiliar with the transaction can understand exactly what happened and why it constitutes a breach.

## Cause of Action for Breach of Contract

Plead the breach of contract claim by establishing each required element with factual support. Demonstrate that a valid and enforceable contract existed by showing mutual assent (offer and acceptance), supported by adequate consideration, between parties with capacity to contract. Identify the specific contractual provisions that the defendant breached, quoting relevant language from the agreement where appropriate. Explain what the defendant was required to do under the contract, how the defendant failed to perform those obligations, and why any excuses or defenses do not apply. Establish that the plaintiff performed all conditions precedent or was excused from performance, and that the plaintiff sustained damages as a direct and proximate result of the defendant's breach. Research and cite the applicable legal standards for breach of contract in your jurisdiction, referencing controlling statutes or leading cases that establish the elements of the claim. Ensure your factual allegations, when accepted as true, satisfy each element of the cause of action under governing law.

## Damages Calculation and Specification

Provide a detailed and well-supported damages calculation. Identify each category of damages being sought, which may include direct damages (the benefit of the bargain), consequential damages (foreseeable losses flowing from the breach), incidental damages (costs incurred in responding to the breach), and any other recoverable losses. For each category, provide specific dollar amounts or good-faith estimates based on the evidence available. Explain the methodology used to calculate damages, whether based on the contract price, market value differentials, actual losses incurred, or other appropriate measures. If seeking consequential damages, establish that such damages were foreseeable at the time of contracting and flow naturally from the breach. Review authoritative resources on damages in purchase agreement breach cases to ensure your damage theories are legally cognizable and properly pleaded. Consider whether the purchase agreement contains any liquidated damages provisions, limitations on damages, or other clauses that affect the available remedies. If the plaintiff incurred costs in attempting to mitigate damages, describe those efforts and associated expenses.

## Prayer for Relief

Draft a comprehensive prayer for relief that requests all appropriate remedies. This should include compensatory damages in a specific amount or according to proof at trial, and may include requests for specific performance if the subject matter of the purchase agreement is unique and monetary damages would be inadequate. Request pre-judgment and post-judgment interest at the applicable legal rate, costs of suit including filing fees and service costs, and reasonable attorney's fees if authorized by the contract or applicable statute. Verify whether the purchase agreement contains an attorney's fees provision and cite it specifically if requesting fee recovery on that basis. Research your jurisdiction's rules regarding what remedies may be requested in a complaint and ensure your prayer conforms to local practice. Some jurisdictions require specific dollar amounts while others permit general prayers for relief; tailor your request accordingly.

## Verification and Signature Requirements

Conclude the complaint with proper verification and signature blocks. Draft a verification statement in which the plaintiff (or an authorized representative with personal knowledge) swears or affirms under penalty of perjury that the allegations in the complaint are true and correct to the best of their knowledge. Research the specific verification requirements in your jurisdiction, as some states require notarization while others accept unsworn declarations under penalty of perjury. Include a complete signature block for the attorney of record, containing the attorney's name, bar number, firm name, complete address, telephone number, email address, and any other information required by local rules. Ensure the signature block complies with the court's formatting requirements and includes all mandatory information.

## Final Document Assembly

Once you have gathered all necessary information from the purchase agreement and supporting documents, researched all applicable legal standards and procedural requirements, and drafted each section with appropriate factual detail and legal support, compile the complete complaint. Ensure the document flows logically, maintains consistent formatting throughout, uses proper legal citation format for any authorities referenced, and presents a compelling and professional pleading that clearly establishes the plaintiff's right to relief. The final complaint should be ready for filing upon attorney review and client approval.
